_comment: Writing assets per build plan §9.10. Variables {route}, {obstacle}, {trait}, {tone}, {recipient}, {power_descriptor} are filled at round-gen time.

rival: The Rival is not using the {route}.
The Rival was seen near the {route} earlier.
obstacle: One of the routes contains {obstacle}, but I cannot tell which.
The {route} is unusually quiet — perhaps too quiet.
preference: {recipient} will not welcome a {tone} approach today.
The court is whispering that {recipient} has been reading {tone} verses.
{recipient} has had a long day; favour {tone} above all.

celeste.name: Lady Celeste
celeste.bio: Reads three books a night and forgives nothing said in haste.

aureon.name: Lord Aureon
aureon.bio: Wins duels he does not mean to fight. Loves anyone who can keep up.

mira.name: Mira the Poet
mira.bio: Believes a kept silence is a love letter in itself.

heir.name: The Masked Heir
heir.bio: Half the court has loved them. None has met them twice.
heir.salutation: To the one behind the mask —

openings: Forgive me. I should never have —
I write because I cannot let yesterday stand.
You should hear it from me, before the court does —
It is past time I admitted —
The west balcony, at the third bell —
Come to the gallery tonight, before the others.
Whatever was said, was said too quickly.
I miss the way you laugh when no one is watching.

hearts.text: The first letter arrives. The recipient smiles, alone, in a corner of the gallery.
A ribbon is returned with no message. It is the answer.
Word travels through the kitchens — quietly, fondly — that the Suitor is not what the court assumed.
At the masquerade, the recipient leaves the dance to find them. The court understands. The court approves.

rumours.text: A fan opens. A name is mouthed behind it.
Two letters arrive at the wrong addresses. Both are read aloud.
By morning, the affair is in three languages and none of them is true.

endings.won: The recipient meets them at the lower gate. No letter is opened. No letter needs to be.
endings.lost: The Rival walks out of the ballroom holding a hand the Suitor never reached.

tutorialPrompts.title: You are not playing alone.
tutorialPrompts.body: Two roles take turns each round: the SUITOR writes the letter and chooses a route; the CONFIDANT sees the truth of the court and helps in silence. You will rotate.

tutorialPrompts.title: You see what they do not.
tutorialPrompts.body: Three private clues this round — about the Rival, the obstacles, and the recipient's mood. Read them carefully. You will not be able to speak any of them aloud.

tutorialPrompts.title: Signals are your only voice.
tutorialPrompts.body: Place three signal tokens on routes (or on tone / intention) using the six symbols. Choose what each symbol MEANS — the Suitor will guess.

tutorialPrompts.title: You also have Action Points.
tutorialPrompts.body: Three AP this round. Cards cost 1 or 2 AP. They cancel obstacles, slow the Rival, or strengthen the letter. Pick a small plan.

tutorialPrompts.title: Look at the signals first.
tutorialPrompts.body: Your Confidant has placed three of them. You won't get to ask much. Read them like a hand of cards.

tutorialPrompts.title: Choose your TONE next.
tutorialPrompts.body: Tender · Bold · Honest · Playful. Each shifts speed and persuasion. Each works only on the right recipient.

tutorialPrompts.title: You have ONE question.
tutorialPrompts.body: Point to a route and ask. Your Confidant answers with one word — Trust, Danger, or Unsure. That is your only direct exchange.

tutorialPrompts.title: Then choose your route, and send the letter.
tutorialPrompts.body: Travel time and letter power are compared against the Rival's. Win to gain a Heart; lose to gain a Rumour.

tutorialPrompts.title: Four Hearts to win. Three Rumours and you are exposed.
tutorialPrompts.body: Roles rotate every round. Whoever you are now, you will not be next round.

tutorialPrompts.title: Court Silence is enforced by the design, not the rules.
tutorialPrompts.body: There is no chat. There is no voice. Resist whispering across the table. The pleasure of this game is in being almost-understood.

questionAnswerLabels.Unsure: I cannot tell. Choose with care.
